mechanism_narrative: A sustained cumulative decline in FEDFUNDS from 5.25% in mid-2007 to 2.0% by August 2008 reflects an active Fed easing cycle that lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as monetary accommodation is priced in.
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
fact:FEDFUNDS fell from 5.25% in Sep2007 to 2.0% by Aug2008
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trailing decline and 20d forward SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX posts negative 20d returns in more than 55% of months during the easing cycle (e.g., Sep-Dec 2008 crisis period).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by 0.4 percentage points or more over a trailing three-month window, it signals a deteriorating labor market consistent with recessionary onset, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised down.
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
fact:UNRATE at 6.1% in Aug2008, up from 5.0% at start of 2008
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d forward returns in more than 55% of instances where the UNRATE trigger fires, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in HOUST (housing starts) and RSAFS (retail sales) indicate a broad-based contraction across both durable (housing) and non-durable (retail) consumer demand channels, which jointly predict negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days more strongly than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month decline AND RSAFS 3-month decline concurrently
evidence_refs: series:HOUST
series:RSAFS
fact:HOUST revised down from 965k to 923k in Jul2008
fact:RSAFS revised down from 381169 to 364611 million in Aug2008
failure_condition: The joint HOUST+RSAFS decline signal fails to outperform the single-series HOUST-only or RSAFS-only signal in out-of-sample Rank IC by any margin over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During an active financial crisis regime characterized by bank failures and interbank funding stress, a turn to negative year-over-year INDPRO growth signals credit-constrained firms cutting production faster than in normal cycles, amplifying negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days beyond the magnitude seen in non-crisis industrial slowdowns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY growth turns negative while financial crisis regime is active
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
fact:INDPRO vintage value 110.30 (Aug2008) later revised to 98.11, indicating sharp industrial contraction
fact:Lehman Brothers collapse and systemic bank stress occurring Sep 2008
failure_condition: 20d forward SPX returns following negative INDPRO YoY prints during the crisis regime are not statistically more negative than during matched non-crisis INDPRO downturns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A persistent month-over-month decline in PAYEMS (nonfarm payrolls) reflects weakening labor demand that triggers immediate risk-off equity positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following each successive payroll decline.
evidence_refs: series:PAYEMS
fact:PAYEMS revised down from 137473k to 137211k for Aug2008, continuing a multi-month decline
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing PAYEMS monthly change and 5d forward SPX returns remains below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already low (<=2%) while CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation remains elevated (>5%), the Fed is constrained from further easing to support growth, creating a stagflation-like policy trap that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as markets price limited monetary support amid inflation risk.
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
series:CPIAUCSL
fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.0% Aug2008
fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value 219.181 Jul2008 reflecting elevated inflation from 2008 oil price spike
failure_condition: SPX 60d forward returns are non-negative (>=0) in more than 50% of periods where both trigger conditions hold jointly, across a 3-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: During an acute financial crisis regime following systemic bank stress events, a month-over-month decline in RSAFS signals a sharper-than-normal contraction in consumer spending due to credit tightening and confidence shocks, producing amplified negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days relative to RSAFS declines observed outside crisis periods.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM decline while financial crisis regime is active
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
fact:RSAFS revised down from 381169 to 364611 million for Aug2008
fact:acute systemic bank stress and Lehman collapse occurring Sep 2008
failure_condition: Negative 20d SPX returns following RSAFS declines during the crisis regime are not statistically distinguishable from returns following equivalent RSAFS declines in non-crisis periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in INDPRO and PAYEMS jointly confirm the onset of a broad economic recession spanning both production and labor markets, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days more reliably than either indicator declining alone.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM decline AND PAYEMS MoM decline concurrently for 2+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
series:PAYEMS
fact:INDPRO vintage 110.30 later revised to 98.11 indicating sharp contraction
fact:PAYEMS declining from 137473k to 137211k in Aug2008
failure_condition: The joint INDPRO+PAYEMS decline signal shows no improvement in Rank IC over the single-series PAYEMS-only signal across any 3-year rolling out-of-sample window.
